thesis: The strongest hackathon demo is not a fake wallet buying random APIs. It is a catalog of agent procurement recipes: a human asks for a useful outcome, the agent buys the cheapest evidence first, stops when confidence or budget fails, and leaves a receipt trail. All recipes below are designed for fake-money or shadow-mode evaluation first; none claims that a live paid x402 call has been completed.

recipes.title: Pay-Per-Contradiction Founder Diligence
recipes.task: Find the cheapest source-backed contradiction in a founder, company, or vendor claim before a meeting.
recipes.human_hook: A tiny agent that says: 'I spent 31 cents and found the one claim you should challenge in the room.'

budget_usd.currency: SIM_USDC for demo; real USDC only after shadow-mode approval

recipes.rail_assumption: x402 is used as a per-call purchase rail where listed providers support it or are represented in the local x402scan atlas. Normal API calls are treated as simulated ingredients when no paid endpoint was verified.

ingredients.name: Exa or Serper search
ingredients.role: Find the public claim surface and alternative sources.
ingredients.evidence_status: simulated provider in fake-money evals; search class appears in local atlas

ingredients.name: Firecrawl or Zyte
ingredients.role: Turn company pages, docs, and announcements into clean evidence.
ingredients.evidence_status: listed in RouteSignal routes database; Firecrawl had strong simulated utility

ingredients.name: GlobalAPI registry and sanctions
ingredients.role: Check entity existence, jurisdiction, and sanctions risk.
ingredients.evidence_status: listed in atlas; simulated as registry and sanctions providers

ingredients.name: Clado/Apollo-style people enrichment
ingredients.role: Verify operators, prior companies, and contact graph.

ingredients.name: twit.sh, StableSocial, or Cascade Surf
ingredients.role: Check recency, claims, launches, and public traction.

ingredients.name: Pinata or 402104
ingredients.role: Store the contradiction bundle and source hashes.
ingredients.evidence_status: listed storage/provenance ingredients; strong simulated ROI

recipes.why_impossible_before: A human could do this with five accounts and a spreadsheet, but an agent could not cheaply decide which paid lookup was worth buying next without pre-funded vendor accounts and manual receipt stitching.
recipes.why_agent_payments_matter: The value is in conditional spend: buy registry only if search finds a real entity, buy people data only if the entity matters, and stop once one high-confidence contradiction is found.
recipes.first_dollar_call: A search or scrape call under $0.01 to establish whether the claim has enough public surface to justify enrichment.
recipes.stop_rule: Stop immediately if two independent discovery calls cannot identify the same entity, if total spend reaches $0.75 without a contradiction, or if sanctions/operator data changes the recommendation to 'do not meet'.
receipt_trail: provider, endpoint class, quoted/simulated price, timestamp, and latency per call
contradiction found or explicit 'no contradiction under budget'
storage URI or hash for the evidence bundle
failure_modes: False contradiction from stale scraped pages.
People enrichment confuses two operators with the same name.
Registry data is jurisdictionally incomplete.
Agent overspends because every weak signal feels worth one more lookup.

feasibility.level: high for fake-money demo; medium for live paid execution
feasibility.demo_status: simulate now with existing provider names, budgets, and receipts
feasibility.honesty_note: No live paid calls or endpoint truth checks are claimed.
feasibility.next_real_check: Run one shadow job on a real company and compare the proposed paid chain to free public evidence before authorizing any spend.

recipes.title: Thin-Liquidity Thesis Ladder
recipes.task: Build a research-only alert for a new token or fee-market event without letting the agent trade.
recipes.human_hook: The agent can say: 'This token is interesting enough for a human to inspect, but I refused to buy alpha after the safety gate failed.'

budget_usd.currency: SIM_USDC for demo; real trades are out of scope

recipes.rail_assumption: x402 or API calls buy research ingredients only. Execution, swaps, transfers, and trade placement require explicit human approval and are not part of this recipe.

ingredients.name: StableCrypto
ingredients.role: Cheap market, token, DefiLlama, CoinGecko, Alchemy, or Etherscan context.
ingredients.evidence_status: listed in atlas and simulated in fake-money evals

ingredients.name: Deepnets.ai
ingredients.role: Token safety, holder graph, funding-network detection, social research.
ingredients.evidence_status: listed in atlas; simulated as safety provider

ingredients.name: Nansen AI
ingredients.role: Smart-money labels and wallet intelligence.
ingredients.evidence_status: listed in atlas; simulated labels provider

ingredients.name: SniperX and Kerdos
ingredients.role: Alpha/new-pair flow plus sentiment and risk analytics.
ingredients.evidence_status: listed in atlas; simulated alpha and risk providers

ingredients.name: OneSource RPC and Satoshi API fees
ingredients.role: Mainnet state and transaction-cost context before any human-approved action.
ingredients.evidence_status: listed or simulated; very cheap in fake-money evals

recipes.why_impossible_before: An occasional crypto researcher needed subscriptions, API keys, and custom scripts across market, label, social, and RPC vendors. The payment rail turns this into a budgeted ladder.
recipes.why_agent_payments_matter: The agent can buy only the next rung when the previous rung clears a threshold. That matters because most token ideas should die after cheap safety checks.
recipes.first_dollar_call: StableCrypto market context or Satoshi fee context, because a cheap market/fee check can invalidate the whole task.
recipes.stop_rule: Stop if safety score fails, holder concentration is extreme, labels indicate obvious wash/rug risk, confidence remains below 0.7 after $0.35, or the user asks for execution.
receipt_trail: per-rung decision and reason for continuing or stopping
provider outputs summarized as research evidence
human-approval gate showing no autonomous trade was made
failure_modes: API outputs are stale relative to fast markets.
The recipe gets mistaken for financial advice.
A social/alpha provider amplifies manipulation.
Human sees a high-confidence alert and over-trusts simulated quality.

feasibility.demo_status: fake-money ladder is already represented by existing eval traces
feasibility.honesty_note: The safe demo is research-only. No autonomous trading or live paid-call quality is proven.
feasibility.next_real_check: Run a no-money shadow ladder on one historical token and compare the stop decision to known later outcomes.

recipes.title: Conference Hallway Radar
recipes.task: Rank who to talk to at a conference and generate one pointed question per target.
recipes.human_hook: A practical party trick: give it the sponsor list, it returns 'talk to this person, ask this, skip that booth'.
recipes.rail_assumption: Use x402-listed search, scrape, social, company, and people ingredients where available; treat outreach as optional and disabled by default.

ingredients.name: x402scan ecosystem/resources
ingredients.role: Seed targets from the agent-payment ecosystem.

ingredients.name: Serper, Exa, Firecrawl
ingredients.role: Find and scrape company surfaces.
ingredients.evidence_status: simulated search/scrape providers with strong fake-money utility

ingredients.name: twit.sh, StableSocial, Cascade Surf, Neynar
ingredients.role: Score social recency and visible builder momentum.

ingredients.name: GlobalAPI and Clado
ingredients.role: Verify company/operator and find the likely person to approach.

ingredients.name: Amalgix or Heurist Deep Research
ingredients.role: Condense evidence into a two-minute conversation brief.

recipes.why_impossible_before: The human version is browser-tab chaos. Agents could search, but paying for one-off people/social/registry calls across vendors was too much setup for a 30-minute conference sprint.
recipes.why_agent_payments_matter: The agent buys just enough context for the next human conversation. The spend is justified by time saved and by avoiding low-signal meetings.
recipes.first_dollar_call: Search/scrape the target's current site or x402scan listing to decide whether deeper company or people enrichment is warranted.
recipes.stop_rule: Stop if public surface is stale, no real operator can be identified, target is not relevant to the current conference question, or the target falls below top-10 after cheap social/search checks.
receipt_trail: source snippets used for each target
one recommended question and one skip reason
failure_modes: The social signal favors loud projects over serious ones.
Person lookup is wrong or invasive.
Conference data is stale.
Agent sends outreach when it should only draft.

feasibility.demo_status: shadow demo works well as a ranking and receipt page; simulated pass rate was weak enough to keep gates tight
feasibility.honesty_note: Conference Scout was not the strongest fake-money flow, so the demo should show stop rules and humility.
feasibility.next_real_check: Run it on a public conference sponsor page without paid calls and ask whether its top five targets are actually useful.

recipes.title: Seller Near-Miss Autopsy
recipes.task: Explain why agents inspect an x402 endpoint and still do not pay.
recipes.human_hook: A seller paste-in report: 'You got crawled. Here are the four missing facts that likely killed conversion.'
recipes.rail_assumption: The agent does not pay the seller endpoint first. It buys or simulates discovery, schema, telemetry, and social trust checks before recommending a paid canary.

ingredients.name: x402scan schema and telemetry
ingredients.role: Check whether the endpoint is discoverable and shows activity-like signals.
ingredients.evidence_status: simulated x402scan providers; local atlas from crawl

ingredients.name: Seller Clinic checks
ingredients.role: Score discovery, schema, sample, price, receipt, failure semantics, trust, and runtime fit.

ingredients.name: Firecrawl scrape
ingredients.role: Inspect docs, examples, pricing copy, and support surface.

ingredients.name: twit.sh or Cascade Surf
ingredients.role: Check whether builders mention failures, confusion, or usage.

ingredients.name: 402104 storage
ingredients.role: Store the autopsy and before/after metadata example.

recipes.why_impossible_before: Sellers could see hits or wallet attempts, but not whether the buyer abandoned because of missing schema, unclear price, low trust, or broken failure semantics.
recipes.why_agent_payments_matter: If agents are the buyers, sellers need machine-readable conversion analytics: what the agent wanted to know before payment and which missing field blocked spend.
recipes.first_dollar_call: x402scan schema/telemetry probe or docs scrape, not a paid call to the seller endpoint itself.
recipes.stop_rule: Stop before any live endpoint payment if schema, sample output, price quote, or receipt semantics are absent.
receipt_trail: readiness score across the eight seller-clinic dimensions
recommended canary call only if readiness clears threshold
failure_modes: Endpoint has private docs not visible to the agent.
Marketplace telemetry numbers are unlabeled and overinterpreted.
Seller treats a simulated autopsy as conversion truth.
The agent flags a missing sample where the seller intentionally withholds one.

feasibility.demo_status: can be rendered from local checklist plus atlas records without claiming endpoint quality
feasibility.honesty_note: This diagnoses public machine-buyability, not true seller conversion unless sellers share logs.
feasibility.next_real_check: Ask one endpoint owner for anonymized probe/payment logs and compare them to the autopsy prediction.

recipes.title: Bangkok Route Reality Desk
recipes.task: Test whether a claimed stablecoin-to-local-commerce route is actually usable and reconcilable.
recipes.human_hook: Not 'crypto payments are coming to SEA' - the agent returns a timestamped route card with fees, limits, KYC, local rail out, and a receipt gap.

budget_usd.currency: SIM_USDC for demo; local paid attestations are future work

recipes.rail_assumption: x402 can pay for evidence, not magically make a local route real. AEON-style claims remain WARN until route proof exists.

ingredients.name: AEON or local commerce payment listing
ingredients.role: Candidate route claim to inspect.
ingredients.evidence_status: listed in atlas as omnichain settlement for real-world merchants

ingredients.name: GlobalAPI economic/regulatory context
ingredients.role: Pull country, sanctions, or economic context relevant to route risk.

ingredients.name: Firecrawl and search
ingredients.role: Collect official provider, regulator, fee, and support pages.

ingredients.name: StableTravel or Maps/places via StableEnrich
ingredients.role: Identify a concrete merchant category or location to verify.

ingredients.name: StableEmail or human attestation placeholder
ingredients.role: Ask for route confirmation, merchant acceptance, or fee quote.

ingredients.name: Pinata or 402104
ingredients.role: Store screenshots, quotes, route steps, and caveats.

recipes.why_impossible_before: Stablecoin route claims were easy to repeat and hard to operationalize. The missing object is a route receipt with asset-in, provider, KYC, limits, fee/spread, local rail out, merchant proof, and failure point.
recipes.why_agent_payments_matter: The agent can buy tiny evidence fragments and local attestations one at a time, only escalating when the official-source check survives.
recipes.first_dollar_call: Search/scrape official provider and regulator pages before contacting any merchant or paying for local proof.
recipes.stop_rule: Stop if the route lacks official provider docs, conflicts with current local rules, cannot name a local rail out, or needs unverifiable human claims before any public proof.
receipt_trail: asset, chain, provider, KYC, limits, fee/spread, and local rail out fields
failure_modes: Regulatory sources change faster than the dataset.
Provider marketing overstates merchant acceptance.
Human attestation is fabricated or not reusable.
The agent confuses settlement availability with spendability.

feasibility.level: medium for a shadow route card; low for live route validation inside the hackathon window
feasibility.demo_status: renderable as a route-proof checklist with one AEON-style example
feasibility.honesty_note: This should be framed as proof-of-route diligence, not a claim that local commerce works today.
feasibility.next_real_check: Pick one city and one merchant category, then require official docs plus a timestamped quote before upgrading from WARN.

recipes.title: Provider Collision Bakeoff
recipes.task: Ask the same question to multiple paid providers and compare cost, latency, freshness, and decision impact.
recipes.human_hook: A judge can watch the agent say: 'Serper found it, Exa added context, Firecrawl gave proof, social disagreed, so I will not buy the expensive provider.'
recipes.rail_assumption: Each provider call is simulated or shadowed first. Live paid calls should start with tiny canary budgets after route logic is proven.

ingredients.name: Exa, Serper, Firecrawl
ingredients.role: Search/scrape collision across public web evidence.
ingredients.evidence_status: simulated providers; Firecrawl and search APIs listed in atlas

ingredients.name: twit.sh, Cascade Surf, StableSocial
ingredients.role: Social collision across X, Reddit, and broader social sources.

ingredients.name: x402scan schema and telemetry
ingredients.role: Provider metadata, activity-like signal, and listing evidence.

ingredients.name: 402104 storage
ingredients.role: Store the bakeoff report and winning-route receipt.

recipes.why_impossible_before: Agents could call one tool, but had no cheap habit of comparing providers as procurement vendors with receipts, stop rules, and outcome scoring.
recipes.why_agent_payments_matter: When each call has a price, provider choice becomes an economic decision. The receipt trail lets future agents route to providers that changed the answer, not just providers that returned data.
recipes.first_dollar_call: Two cheapest search providers in parallel, then stop if they agree and the confidence threshold is met.
recipes.stop_rule: Stop after two independent providers agree above threshold, or after three providers disagree in a way that requires human review, or after $0.30 without a decision delta.
receipt_trail: same input sent to each provider
price, latency, success, freshness, and quality score
whether the call changed the decision
failure_modes: Providers share upstream sources, creating false independence.
Latency and cost are simulated, not observed live.
Quality scores reward verbose outputs rather than correct ones.
The agent overfits to one benchmark question.

feasibility.level: high for demo and strongest simulated flow
feasibility.demo_status: fake-money eval reported 98 percent pass rate and highest product score among tested flows
feasibility.honesty_note: Provider quality was mocked; the live truthfulness claim is unproven.
feasibility.next_real_check: Run one real no-payment provider collision on public docs, then one sub-$0.25 paid canary only if schemas and receipts are clear.

recipes.title: Counterparty Risk Preflight Before Agent Spend
recipes.task: Screen a seller, wallet, domain, or company before an agent is allowed to pay it.
recipes.human_hook: The agent pauses at the cash register and says: 'I can pay, but this seller has no operator identity and no failure policy.'
recipes.rail_assumption: x402 payment intent triggers screening before the paid call proceeds. x402 is the payment challenge; policy, KYA/KYT, and failure rules live around it.

ingredients.name: GlobalAPI registry and sanctions
ingredients.role: Check company, jurisdiction, and sanctions surface.

ingredients.name: Vishwa, AgentLux, AgentRanking-style trust layers
ingredients.role: Represent identity/reputation/KYA surfaces for future scoring.
ingredients.evidence_status: listed in atlas or Reddit pain-signal watchlist

ingredients.name: x402scan and marketplace metadata
ingredients.role: Check listing, interfaces, and public activity-like signals.

ingredients.name: Firecrawl
ingredients.role: Inspect docs for operator identity, privacy, support, and refund/failure semantics.

ingredients.name: Pinata or 402104
ingredients.role: Store policy decision and audit receipt.

recipes.why_impossible_before: Payment happened through human accounts and platform trust. Agent payments need a programmable preflight because the buyer may be an unattended runtime.
recipes.why_agent_payments_matter: The moment an agent can pay an unknown endpoint, every call becomes a procurement and compliance decision. Cheap preflight can block expensive or unsafe spend.
recipes.first_dollar_call: A free or near-free metadata/schema scrape. Do not pay the target endpoint until the policy preflight returns PROBE or PAY.
recipes.stop_rule: Block if seller identity is absent for high-risk tasks, sanctions risk is present, failure semantics are missing for nontrivial spend, or endpoint asks for more than the task cap.
receipt_trail: audit receipt stored before any payment
failure_modes: Trust signals are gameable.
False positives block small benign endpoints.
Jurisdictional data is incomplete.
Developers misread a preflight as legal advice.

feasibility.demo_status: strong as a policy decision card; live compliance validity is out of scope
feasibility.honesty_note: This is risk triage and audit logging, not legal or sanctions compliance certification.
feasibility.next_real_check: Run against three public x402 listings: one strong identity, one weak identity, one no-description endpoint.

recipes.title: Meme-to-Merch Microstore
recipes.task: Detect a niche social trend, produce one merch idea, and prepare an agent-buyable checkout without running a full storefront.
recipes.human_hook: A weird but legible demo: the agent finds a micro-community joke and turns it into one pay-per-agent shirt with receipts.
recipes.rail_assumption: x402 is used for pay-per-call social data, generation or merch checkout where listed. Real product purchase is disabled unless the user approves.

ingredients.name: twit.sh, StableSocial, Cascade Surf, or Neynar
ingredients.role: Find trend phrases, community jokes, and recency signal.

ingredients.name: StableMerch
ingredients.role: Agent-purchasable custom shirt endpoint.

ingredients.name: Bonsai or model gateway
ingredients.role: Generate/remix a design or content concept.
ingredients.evidence_status: listed in atlas; design generation simulated

ingredients.name: StableEmail
ingredients.role: Draft launch note or notify a small opt-in list.

ingredients.name: LoyalSpark or tip.md
ingredients.role: Represent loyalty/tip/reward loop after purchase.

ingredients.name: Pinata
ingredients.role: Store trend evidence, design prompt, and checkout receipt.

recipes.why_impossible_before: A one-off merch experiment required social scraping, design tooling, checkout setup, email, loyalty, and storage accounts. That is too much overhead for a micro-trend.
recipes.why_agent_payments_matter: Per-call access makes silly experiments economically rational: the agent buys just enough trend evidence and checkout setup to test one product, then stops.
recipes.first_dollar_call: Social pulse call to prove the phrase is current and not a hallucinated joke.
recipes.stop_rule: Stop if fewer than two sources show current social signal, if merch quote exceeds cap, if copyright/trademark risk appears, or if the user has not approved real checkout.
receipt_trail: design prompt and generated asset hash
checkout quote but no purchase unless approved
failure_modes: Trend signal is too thin or platform-specific.
Generated design violates rights or taste boundaries.
Agent buys merch nobody wants.
Social APIs return noisy engagement bait.

feasibility.demo_status: fun visual/product recipe; should remain a quote-and-plan demo unless real checkout is verified
feasibility.honesty_note: This is more speculative than diligence or provider benchmarking, but useful for showing non-boring commerce loops.
feasibility.next_real_check: Verify StableMerch schema, sample output, price quote, receipt, and cancellation behavior before any purchase.

recipes.title: Receipt Quilt for Agent Research
recipes.task: Turn a multi-provider research answer into a durable bundle of paid-call receipts and source evidence.
recipes.human_hook: Instead of trusting the answer, the human opens the receipt quilt: every tile is a call, price, source, and reason it mattered.
recipes.rail_assumption: Every paid or simulated call emits a receipt object even when the provider does not. x402 transaction proof is not treated as proof of output quality.

ingredients.name: Pinata and 402104
ingredients.role: Store raw and summarized evidence bundles.

ingredients.name: Prove API / Polymer
ingredients.role: Represent proof generation and query for claims needing stronger attestations.

ingredients.name: x402scan telemetry
ingredients.role: Link endpoint/payment observations to the receipt ledger.

ingredients.name: Amalgix or Heurist
ingredients.role: Summarize evidence without losing source references.

ingredients.name: AgentLux or Vishwa
ingredients.role: Attach identity/trust context to who bought or sold the call.

recipes.why_impossible_before: Receipts were scattered across API dashboards, wallet transactions, and local notes. Agents need portable memory of what was bought and whether it changed the answer.
recipes.why_agent_payments_matter: Payment creates a natural audit event. If captured well, each paid call becomes reusable provenance for future agents instead of disposable spend.
recipes.first_dollar_call: A storage/provenance call after the first meaningful evidence source, not after the whole task is over.
recipes.stop_rule: Stop storing raw output that includes secrets or personal data, stop proof escalation if source confidence is low, and cap storage/proof spend below 20 percent of task budget.
receipt_trail: input hash and output summary hash
price, latency, success, and decision delta
storage URI and optional proof/job id
failure_modes: Receipts leak sensitive inputs.
Storage proves existence, not truth.
Proof calls are overkill for cheap research.
Provider receipts use incompatible formats.

feasibility.level: high as a local schema and demo object
feasibility.demo_status: can be rendered from fake receipts immediately
feasibility.honesty_note: Storage/proof receipts do not prove endpoint correctness or customer demand.
feasibility.next_real_check: Define one receipt schema and attach it to a single shadow provider collision run.

recipes.title: Node Package Security Microaudit
recipes.task: Before an agent installs a package or MCP server, buy a tiny security and provenance audit.
recipes.human_hook: The agent refuses to install a package until it has bought one cheap scan and stored the risk receipt.
recipes.rail_assumption: Use x402-style pay-per-scan where available; otherwise simulate the paid scanner and require human approval before install.

ingredients.name: security.togoder.click-style scanner
ingredients.role: Paid Node package security scan referenced in r/x402 corpus.
ingredients.evidence_status: Reddit lead, not present as verified local endpoint

ingredients.name: Firecrawl
ingredients.role: Fetch package docs, repo page, install notes, and warnings.

ingredients.name: GlobalAPI or sanctions/company check
ingredients.role: Optional operator/domain context for higher-risk packages.

ingredients.name: x402scan or MCP registry metadata
ingredients.role: Check whether a paid MCP/tool endpoint is discoverable and runtime-fit.
ingredients.evidence_status: local x402scan; MCP registry mentioned in Reddit pain signals

ingredients.name: 402104 storage
ingredients.role: Store audit receipt and install decision.

recipes.why_impossible_before: Package installs were either manual trust or broad platform scanners. Agent runtimes need tiny just-in-time checks before tool installation.
recipes.why_agent_payments_matter: A one-off security check can be worth pennies when the alternative is granting an agent runtime access to a malicious dependency or MCP server.
recipes.first_dollar_call: Paid package scan only after free docs/repo scrape confirms the package is actually under consideration.
recipes.stop_rule: Block install if scanner flags high severity, package has no clear source, endpoint lacks runtime fit, or audit cannot be stored.
receipt_trail: install decision and human approval status
failure_modes: Scanner has shallow coverage.
Package risk changes after the scan.
Agent grants permissions beyond what the audit considered.
Reddit lead is not a live reliable provider.

feasibility.demo_status: can be a simulated install gate with a placeholder paid scanner
feasibility.honesty_note: The named scanner is a Reddit lead, not a locally verified paid endpoint.
feasibility.next_real_check: Find or build one real scanner endpoint with schema, sample, price quote, and deterministic output on a known package.

recipes.title: No-Account Data Room for a Long-Stay Asset
recipes.task: Create a small diligence data room for an obscure hotel, guesthouse, coliving, or retreat property.
recipes.human_hook: The agent turns 'could this place be interesting?' into a source-backed memo with owner, demand, route, complaints, and missing proof.
recipes.rail_assumption: Per-call paid ingredients replace signing up for many travel, maps, registry, social, scrape, and storage APIs. Outreach and payments are draft-only.

ingredients.name: StableTravel
ingredients.role: Travel, hotel, activity, or transfer search ingredient.

ingredients.name: StableEnrich / Google Maps-style places
ingredients.role: Place details, public footprint, and local business context.
ingredients.evidence_status: listed in atlas as access to Google Maps and enrichment APIs

ingredients.name: Firecrawl and search
ingredients.role: Scrape website, reviews, booking pages, and local articles.

ingredients.name: GlobalAPI registry/sanctions
ingredients.role: Check operator, country, company, and risk context.

ingredients.name: StableSocial / Cascade Surf
ingredients.role: Find recent guest complaints or hidden local signal.

ingredients.name: StableEmail
ingredients.role: Draft owner/operator questions without sending by default.

ingredients.name: Pinata / 402104
ingredients.role: Archive the data room and proof ledger.

recipes.why_impossible_before: The asset is too small for a formal data vendor and too weird for one subscription. The useful workflow needs many tiny facts from many providers.
recipes.why_agent_payments_matter: The agent can buy only the missing fact needed to move from curiosity to 'visit', 'email', 'avoid', or 'needs local proof'.
recipes.first_dollar_call: Place/search lookup to decide whether the asset has enough public evidence to justify operator and social checks.
recipes.stop_rule: Stop if the property cannot be uniquely identified, if owner/operator cannot be separated from public-web reachability, or if complaint/safety signal exceeds threshold.
receipt_trail: public-web evidence versus owner/diligence verified fields
failure_modes: The agent mistakes OTA listings for owner evidence.
Reviews are stale or manipulated.
Local legal/property facts require human due diligence.
Outreach draft implies readiness that the data does not support.

feasibility.demo_status: good long-tail recipe for showing human-enjoyable use beyond pure crypto
feasibility.honesty_note: This is public-evidence assembly. It must not imply owner verification or deal readiness.
feasibility.next_real_check: Run a shadow data room for one known property and mark every field as public-web, inferred, or verified.

recipes.title: Agent-Bought Ad Cell Market Test
recipes.task: Let an agent evaluate, buy, or decline one tiny local/content ad cell based on audience and receipt quality.
recipes.human_hook: A delightfully odd demo: the agent can buy one pixel/square/ad cell only if it can explain why the audience is real.

budget_usd.currency: SIM_USDC for demo; real purchase disabled by default

recipes.rail_assumption: LocalSquare-style ad cells are a Reddit-observed non-API commerce pattern. Treat it as speculative until endpoint schema, audience proof, price, and receipt semantics are verified.

ingredients.name: LocalSquare-style ad cell
ingredients.role: Candidate agent-purchasable media slot.
ingredients.evidence_status: Reddit lead, not locally verified as a live endpoint

ingredients.name: StableSocial, twit.sh, Neynar
ingredients.role: Check audience, topic fit, and social recency around the cell/community.

ingredients.name: Firecrawl
ingredients.role: Scrape the ad page, terms, pricing, and display proof.

ingredients.name: GlobalAPI or domain/operator lookup
ingredients.role: Check operator surface for higher spend.

ingredients.name: Pinata
ingredients.role: Store creative, quote, purchase decision, and screenshot/receipt.

recipes.why_impossible_before: Tiny ad buys were not worth human setup, cards, accounts, and reconciliation. Agent payments make the buy technically possible, but audience proof is still the bottleneck.
recipes.why_agent_payments_matter: The agent can buy attention in tiny increments if it can prove the slot, audience, and receipt before spend.
recipes.first_dollar_call: Scrape and social audience check. Do not buy the ad cell as the first dollar.
recipes.stop_rule: Stop if audience evidence is not independently visible, operator identity is unclear, quote lacks display/receipt terms, or user has not approved real purchase.
failure_modes: Audience numbers are fake or unlabeled.
The ad cell exists but has no meaningful distribution.
Agent buys novelty instead of value.
Receipt proves purchase, not attention.

feasibility.demo_status: interesting edge-case recipe; should be marked speculative
feasibility.honesty_note: LocalSquare is a pain-signal/example lead, not verified local endpoint evidence.
feasibility.next_real_check: Verify one actual ad-cell endpoint with schema, price, audience evidence, and receipt before showing a purchase button.

recipes.title: Long-Running Job Escrow Router
recipes.task: Decide whether a paid task should use instant x402, quote-first negotiation, or an escrow/state protocol.
recipes.human_hook: The agent refuses to pay upfront for 'hours-long research' through a one-shot 402 call and asks for a job id or escrow instead.
recipes.rail_assumption: x402 is appropriate for immediate request-response calls. Long-running jobs need a second layer for job id, delivery verification, escrow, refund, dispute, or result token.

ingredients.name: Seller Clinic failure semantics
ingredients.role: Check whether paid output can fail after settlement.

ingredients.name: ACTP / Agirails-style escrow boundary
ingredients.role: Represent stateful paid work above x402.

ingredients.name: Prove API / Polymer
ingredients.role: Example of async proof request and status query pattern.

ingredients.name: x402scan schema
ingredients.role: Inspect whether endpoint appears request-response or job-based.

ingredients.name: Pinata / 402104
ingredients.role: Store the routing decision and lifecycle receipt.

recipes.why_impossible_before: The protocol conversation often collapsed all payments into one rail. Agents need to distinguish a data lookup from delayed work with delivery risk.
recipes.why_agent_payments_matter: The agent can pay instantly for small verified outputs while refusing to misuse x402 for tasks that need state, escrow, or proof of delivery.
recipes.first_dollar_call: Schema or sample probe. The first dollar should classify lifecycle risk, not buy the long job.
recipes.stop_rule: Do not pay one-shot x402 if the result is delayed, ambiguous, subjective, or not recoverable after provider failure.
receipt_trail: job id, result token, or block reason
failure_modes: Seller hides delayed work behind an instant endpoint.
Escrow layer is unavailable or experimental.
Agent blocks useful async jobs too aggressively.
Receipt captures process but not deliverable quality.

feasibility.level: high as a policy/router card; medium as a live integration
feasibility.demo_status: works as a decision recipe without needing live endpoint payments
feasibility.honesty_note: Escrow/state rails are referenced as adjacent protocol needs, not implemented here.
feasibility.next_real_check: Classify 10 atlas endpoints by instant versus delayed deliverable and inspect docs for job_id/result-token patterns.

recipes.title: Free Subgraph Discovery to Paid Query Loop
recipes.task: Use free discovery to choose a subgraph or onchain dataset, then pay only for the query that matters.
recipes.human_hook: The agent browses for free, pays only when it knows the exact query, then stores the query receipt for repeatability.
recipes.rail_assumption: This follows the free-discovery/paid-execution pattern surfaced in the r/x402 corpus. Local recipe is a shadow-mode model until The Graph gateway details are verified in current docs.

ingredients.name: subgraph-registry-mcp-style discovery
ingredients.role: Find candidate subgraphs or datasets without payment.
ingredients.evidence_status: Reddit lead; not normalized as a local atlas record

ingredients.name: The Graph x402 gateway-style paid query
ingredients.role: Pay per query after target dataset is selected.
ingredients.evidence_status: Reddit lead; requires live verification before paid demo

ingredients.name: StableCrypto or OneSource RPC
ingredients.role: Cross-check onchain context or live state.

ingredients.name: Satoshi API fees
ingredients.role: Estimate execution/network timing where relevant.

ingredients.name: Pinata / 402104
ingredients.role: Store query, result hash, and repeatability receipt.

recipes.why_impossible_before: Discovery and execution were bundled into subscriptions or developer accounts. The cleaner pattern is free inventory, then a precise paid query.
recipes.why_agent_payments_matter: Agents should not pay to browse. They should pay when the query is known, bounded, and likely to change the decision.
recipes.first_dollar_call: The first paid call is the selected query, after free discovery has identified dataset, fields, and expected value.
recipes.stop_rule: Stop if free discovery cannot identify the right subgraph, if query cost is unclear, if result schema is unknown, or if a cached prior receipt already answers the question.
failure_modes: Discovery registry is stale.
Paid query returns a schema mismatch.
Agent pays for duplicate queries that should have been cached.
Live gateway differs from Reddit-described pattern.

feasibility.demo_status: strong conceptual recipe; should be labeled Reddit-derived until gateway is checked
feasibility.honesty_note: No live The Graph paid query was performed in this artifact.
feasibility.next_real_check: Verify current gateway endpoint, discovery MCP, price challenge, sample query, and receipt semantics before any paid run.

generation_notes: All budgets are demo budgets and default to SIM_USDC fake-money or shadow-mode execution.
The dataset intentionally mixes practical recipes with weird long-tail commerce and provenance examples.

Coverage includes diligence, onchain trading research, conference scouting, seller audit, route/local commerce reality, provider collision benchmark, compliance/risk, content/social intelligence, storage/provenance, package security, ad-cell commerce, long-running job routing, and free-discovery/paid-query loops.
Evidence refs point to local artifacts and atlas records; they are not proof of live endpoint quality.
No recipe claims live paid x402 calls, real transaction completion, legal compliance certification, or financial advice.
